              object_to_subjects[t.object] = []
          object_to_subjects[t.object].append(t.subject)
  
      # Identify which subjects are also objects (can be chained)
      chainable_subjects = set(object_to_subjects.keys()) & set(subject_triples.keys())
  
      def build_sentence_for_subject(subj, visited=None):
          """Build sentence starting from a subject, chaining to related subjects."""
          if visited is None:
              visited = set()
          if subj in visited:
              return ""
          visited.add(subj)
  
          if subj not in subject_to_triples:
              return ""
  
          tlist = subject_to_triples[subj]
          subj_clauses = [phrase(t) for t in tlist]

with:
  # Build a single coherent sentence from all triples
      # Group triples by subject for better sentence construction
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Use the first triple's subject as the main subject
      main_subject = triples[0].subject
  
      # Build clauses for the main subject
      main_clauses = []
      other_clauses = []
  
      for t in triples:
          if t.subject == main_subject:
              # Create clause without repeating the subject
              p = t.predicate.strip().lower()
              obj = t.object
  
              # Build natural phrases for each predicate
              if p == "discoverer":
                  clause = f"was discovered by {obj}"
              elif p == "discovered":
                  clause = f"was discovered on {obj}"

                  clause = f"has an epoch of {obj}"
